paper.title: Quantifying Event-Driven Demand Perturbations in Load Forecasting Using News-Aware Representation Learning
paper.subtitle: News-Aware Context-Balancing Framework for counterfactual load forecasting

No_News.label: No news baseline
No_News.description: A low-intensity window with no extracted demand-relevant events.

Env_Thermal.label: Thermal event
Env_Thermal.description: Heat and temperature stress signals that often alter cooling demand.

Env_Disaster.label: Natural disaster
Env_Disaster.description: Bushfires, storms, floods, and infrastructure-disruption weather events.

Soc_Calendar.label: Calendar event
Soc_Calendar.description: Holidays, public events, and scheduled activity changes.

Soc_Economic.label: Socio-economic event
Soc_Economic.description: Business activity, policy, restriction, and demand-response signals.

Grid_Reliability.label: Grid reliability
Grid_Reliability.description: Reserve scarcity, outages, load shedding, and system warnings.

events.text: Bureau of Meteorology warns a severe heatwave will build across southern and a larger area of NSW from Thursday into next week, with some areas forecast to reach extreme heatwave conditions.
events.justification: Forecast extreme/high temperatures across much of NSW will drive large increases in cooling (HVAC) demand and peak load.

events.text: An 'extreme' heatwave is forecast to develop across large parts of NSW from the weekend into early next week, with temperatures into the 40s in parts of the mainland and mid-30s elsewhere, raising cooling demand and fire/smoke risk (likely smoke haze in Sydney on New Year's Eve).
events.justification: Forecasted extreme temperatures across much of NSW will materially increase residential and commercial HVAC loads and peak demand; accompanying fire/smoke risks can also alter usage patterns.

events.text: At 08:30 there were 72 bush or grass fires burning across NSW, with 30 not yet contained and very high fire danger ratings across multiple regions (including areas from the far South Coast up to Sydney).
events.justification: Widespread active bushfires and elevated fire danger can threaten transmission/distribution infrastructure, alter local demand (evacuations, emergency services) and increase uncertainty for supply.

events.text: Drought and bushfire crisis in NSW (Byron Bay region) causing potable water shortages and use of water trucks; highlighted in reporting about Byron Bay residents.
events.justification: Drought and active bushfire conditions represent an environmental disaster risk that can stress infrastructure, increase emergency and pumping loads, and threaten generation/transmission assets in NSW.

events.text: Boxing Day sales drove large in-person retail activity across NSW (Sydney CBD, Homebush, North Sydney and regional centres), producing higher daytime commercial electricity use at shopping centres and stores.
events.justification: Boxing Day is a predictable, statewide retail event with reported large crowds and extended shopping activity in multiple NSW centres, raising commercial/retail demand for electricity during the day.

events.text: Widespread bushfires burning across NSW — 80 fires reported with 35 burning out of control; 21 US firefighting personnel arrived in Sydney to assist and nine total fire bans are in place.
events.justification: Large, uncontrolled bushfires across NSW create direct risks to transmission and distribution assets, can force local load reductions or outages, and raise operational firefighting demands — all materially affecting state electricity demand and supply reliability.

events.text: Widespread bushfires affecting NSW with smoke over Sydney and federal/state leaders calling for a national response; Prime Minister visited NSW to thank firefighters actively working in the field.
events.justification: Article reports active bushfires and smoke impacting Sydney/NSW and on-the-ground firefighting activity; such disasters pose physical risks to transmission/distribution and drive emergency-related demand shifts and potential outages.

events.text: Active bushfires across the east coast with Sydney experiencing hazardous smoke levels, impacting NSW communities.
events.justification: Ongoing bushfires and heavy smoke in Sydney can damage/strain transmission infrastructure, force evacuations and emergency services, and alter residential/commercial electricity consumption patterns across NSW.

events.text: Warnings and analysis indicating unprecedented bushfire risk for the coming summer, with experts saying the worst may be yet to come.
events.justification: Forecasted elevated bushfire risk increases the likelihood of future demand shocks and supply disruptions in NSW (increased emergency/residential load, potential outages and network impacts).

events.text: Heatwave conditions are forecast for large parts of inland NSW with temperatures set to reach the low 40s, increasing air-conditioning and cooling load.
events.justification: Forecasted extreme heat (low-40s) will drive elevated residential and commercial cooling demand across NSW, raising peak load risk and impacting short-term demand forecasts.

events.text: New South Wales bushfires produced hazardous smoke over Sydney and nearby areas, triggering office and university closures, evacuations, ferry cancellations and public health advisories to stay indoors.
events.justification: Active NSW bushfires and extreme smoke caused widespread disruptions and closures, shifting demand away from commercial/transport sectors toward residential HVAC/indoor air filtration and posing physical risk to infrastructure.

events.text: Bureau of Meteorology warns a severe heatwave will build across southern and a larger area of NSW from Thursday into next week, with some areas forecast to reach extreme heatwave conditions.
events.justification: Forecast extreme/high temperatures across much of NSW will drive large increases in cooling (HVAC) demand and peak load.

events.text: An 'extreme' heatwave is forecast to develop across large parts of NSW from the weekend into early next week, with temperatures into the 40s in parts of the mainland and mid-30s elsewhere, raising cooling demand and fire/smoke risk (likely smoke haze in Sydney on New Year's Eve).
events.justification: Forecasted extreme temperatures across much of NSW will materially increase residential and commercial HVAC loads and peak demand; accompanying fire/smoke risks can also alter usage patterns.

events.text: At 08:30 there were 72 bush or grass fires burning across NSW, with 30 not yet contained and very high fire danger ratings across multiple regions (including areas from the far South Coast up to Sydney).
events.justification: Widespread active bushfires and elevated fire danger can threaten transmission/distribution infrastructure, alter local demand (evacuations, emergency services) and increase uncertainty for supply.

events.text: Drought and bushfire crisis in NSW (Byron Bay region) causing potable water shortages and use of water trucks; highlighted in reporting about Byron Bay residents.
events.justification: Drought and active bushfire conditions represent an environmental disaster risk that can stress infrastructure, increase emergency and pumping loads, and threaten generation/transmission assets in NSW.

events.text: Boxing Day sales with large expected shopper turnout (national figure cited) and retailers running major in-store promotions — increased commercial demand in Sydney CBD and wider NSW retail precincts.
events.justification: Boxing Day is a deterministic calendar event driving concentrated retail activity and footfall; large crowds and extended trading raise commercial electricity use (lighting, HVAC, POS systems) in Sydney/NSW.

events.text: Boxing Day sales drove large in-person retail activity across NSW (Sydney CBD, Homebush, North Sydney and regional centres), producing higher daytime commercial electricity use at shopping centres and stores.
events.justification: Boxing Day is a predictable, statewide retail event with reported large crowds and extended shopping activity in multiple NSW centres, raising commercial/retail demand for electricity during the day.

events.text: NSW experienced a heatwave with Sydney reaching around 36C, prompting NSW Ambulance warnings and elevated heat-health risk — driving higher residential and commercial cooling demand across the state.
events.justification: Observed extreme temperatures (36C) directly increase HVAC cooling load statewide; ambulance warnings and public-health alerts indicate elevated demand impacts.

events.text: Widespread bushfires across NSW — around 45 active blazes, more than 500,000 hectares burned this year, 500+ firefighters and 40–50 aircraft committed — creating ongoing fire danger and potential impacts on infrastructure and localized electricity supply and demand.
events.justification: Active, large-area fires threaten transmission/distribution infrastructure, may force outages or constrain supply and drive emergency services load, thus materially affecting NSW electricity balance.

events.text: A cold front is forecast to move over NSW this weekend, with Sydney temperatures expected to fall to around 24C by Sunday, which would reduce cooling demand relative to peak heat days.
events.justification: Forecasted temperature decrease implies lower HVAC usage and reduced peak demand, though persistence of winds may sustain fire risk which could moderate the demand reduction.

events.text: NSW government has passed planning laws for short-term holiday letting: hosts present can let year-round; where hosts are absent properties in greater Sydney are limited to 180 days/year (outside Sydney councils can limit but not below 180 days, Byron Shire can impose 90 days).
events.justification: State policy changes the allowable amount of short-stay accommodation across NSW, shifting residential occupancy and tourist nights that alter residential and commercial electricity consumption patterns, particularly in greater Sydney and tourist regions.

events.text: Aqualand has multiple Sydney developments including a current project of 415 luxury apartments at 168 Walker Street, North Sydney.
events.justification: The article reports confirmed property development activity in North Sydney; new residential capacity will increase long-term residential electricity demand in NSW.

events.text: FOMO Festival 2020 will include a Parramatta Park (Sydney, NSW) show on January 11, 2020 — a large scheduled music festival likely to increase local electricity demand for stage lighting, PA systems, vendor operations and crowd services.
events.justification: Scheduled large public event in NSW (Parramatta Park) will create concentrated daytime/evening loads (lighting, sound, concessions, cooling), affecting local demand profiles.

events.text: Severe storms in Sydney damaged Ausgrid power lines and poles, leaving thousands of homes and businesses without electricity for a fifth day; about 9,000 customers remained without power while ~42,500 had been restored; crews from other networks were mobilised and unions say distributor workforce cuts have slowed repairs.
events.justification: Confirmed distribution network damage and prolonged large-scale outages in Sydney directly constrain supply and alter local demand patterns; reduced frontline crew capacity prolongs restoration.

events.text: Fire Weather Warning issued and 147 fires burning across NSW (57 uncontained), with severe wind and storm warnings and forecast storms over the weekend that could worsen fire and storm impacts.
events.justification: Active bushfires and an official fire-weather warning present ongoing risk to transmission/distribution infrastructure and can drive demand changes through evacuations, emergency response loads, and outage-driven shifts.

events.text: Inner-city Sydney properties (Woolloomooloo, Newtown, Kings Cross) formerly operating as brothels are being sold and converted into boarding houses and accommodation to meet high housing demand, increasing residential occupancy in these suburbs.
events.justification: Conversion of commercial premises into higher-density residential boarding houses raises local residential electricity consumption (hot water, cooking, lighting, HVAC) in inner-Sydney neighborhoods.
